13.reason: Global variable l is not reinitialized before the second call to bfs(). After the first BFS, l retains its final value, causing this line to write to an incorrect queue index. Additionally, f is not reset, so the while loop may not execute correctly.

23.reason: should mark visited when enqueuing to avoid duplicate entries; missing vis[e[tmp][j]] = 1;

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  should mark visited when enqueuing to avoid duplicate entries; missing vis[e[tmp][j]] = 1;
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Nodes are not marked as visited when enqueued, allowing duplicates to enter the queue. This can cause queue overflow (size 101) and incorrect traversal order. Visited status should be set here.

32.reason: [The comparison function does not handle equality correctly. It should return 0 when *aa == *bb, but currently returns -1, which violates the requirements of qsort and may cause undefined behavior or incorrect sorting.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  comparator does not return 0 for equal elements; should handle equality (e.g., return *aa - *bb).
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The comparison function does not handle equality correctly. It should return 0 when *aa == *bb, but currently returns -1, which violates the requirements of qsort and may cause undefined behavior or incorrect sorting.]

56.reason: Global queue pointers f and l are not reset to 0 and -1 before calling BFS. After the first traversal, f > l, causing the loop condition l>=f to fail immediately on subsequent calls. f and l must be reset before each BFS invocation.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Global queue pointers f and l are not reset to 0 and -1 before calling BFS. After the first traversal, f > l, causing the loop condition l>=f to fail immediately on subsequent calls. f and l must be reset before each BFS invocation.

66.reason: Same issue: f and l retain their values from the previous BFS call, so this traversal will not execute correctly. Reset f=0; l=-1; here as well.
